summary: Iteration 18: Agent routing system testing. All 24 backend tests passed. services/agents.py (4-agent AGENTS dict) and services/agent_router.py (rule-based + Claude-haiku fallback) are fully functional and importable. MessageResponse schema correctly includes agent_type and agent_name as Optional[str]=None (backward compatible). POST /api/chats/{chat_id}/messages correctly routes to an agent and persists agent_type/agent_name in the assistant message. GET /api/chats/{chat_id}/messages returns agent_type=None for old messages without breaking schema validation.
passed_tests: GET /api/health returns 200 with status=healthy (no import errors from new services)
POST /api/auth/login with admin@ai.planetworkspace.com / Admin@123456 returns token + user
services/agents.py imports cleanly: AGENTS dict has all 4 agents (excel, research, rag, general)
Each agent has 'name' and 'system_prompt' fields with non-trivial content
get_agent('excel'/'research'/'rag'/'general') returns correct agent dict
get_agent('unknown_type') falls back to general agent (correct behavior)
services/agent_router.py imports cleanly: route_to_agent is a callable async function
route_to_agent(has_excel_source=True, 'excel' keyword in message) → 'excel'
route_to_agent(has_rag_context=True) → 'rag'
route_to_agent(use_web_search=True) → 'research'
route_to_agent(no context, no api key) → returns valid agent type (general fallback)
route_to_agent(very long message edge case) → returns valid agent type (no crash)
MessageResponse.agent_type: Optional[str]=None field confirmed
MessageResponse.agent_name: Optional[str]=None field confirmed
MessageResponse constructed without agent_type/agent_name → backward compatible (both None)
MessageResponse accepts all 4 valid agent_type strings
GET /api/chats/7d34ab68-60b5-44d4-9468-cf8f93fead5b/messages returns 200
All messages pass schema validation (id, chatId, role, content, createdAt present)
Old assistant messages have agent_type=None (no schema errors, backward compatible)
POST /api/chats/{project_chat}/messages returns agent_type in {excel,research,rag,general}
POST /api/chats/{project_chat}/messages returns non-empty agent_name
After POST, GET messages shows persisted agent_type matching the response
POST without auth returns 401/403 (not 200)
Quick chat: create + send message → agent_type in valid set, agent_name non-empty, cleanup successful
critical_code_review_comments: INFO: agent_router.py uses synchronous anthropic.Anthropic client inside an async function (route_to_agent). The synchronous Claude haiku call will block the event loop. For production, consider using AsyncAnthropic client (anthropic.AsyncAnthropic). However, this is non-critical since the fallback to 'general' works correctly and the rule-based routing (which is async-safe) covers most cases.

INFO: The Claude haiku model name used is 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' — verify this is the correct model identifier. If invalid, the exception handler catches it and falls back to 'general' (no crash).

INFO: agent_router.py has a minor Russian/Armenian keyword mix in research_keywords that includes 'гтир', 'фнтрир' which appear to be garbled characters. Unlikely to match in practice but non-breaking.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TEST_AgentRouting QuickChat during test run (cleaned up). Sent 3 messages to project chat 7d34ab68-60b5-44d4-9468-cf8f93fead5b.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Agent routing fully verified. Key IDs: project=560694a9-6522-4dda-bc5e-f8d81f329cbe, project chat=7d34ab68-60b5-44d4-9468-cf8f93fead5b. New fields: agent_type (Optional[str]=None), agent_name (Optional[str]=None) in MessageResponse. Rule-based routing: has_excel_source+excel_keyword→excel, has_rag_context→rag, use_web_search→research, fallback→general. Claude-haiku call in route_to_agent uses sync client (minor async concern but non-blocking in practice). Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_agent_routing.py.
